CONTRACT-001: DDx / HELIX Boundary Contract\n\n**Status:** Draft  \n**Owner:** HELIX maintainers  \n**Related:** [Product Vision](../../00-discover/product-vision.md), [FEAT-011](../../01-frame/features/FEAT-011-slider-autonomy.md), [ADR-001](../adr/ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md)\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis contract defines the ownership boundary between **DDx** and **HELIX**.\n\n- **DDx** is the platform substrate.\n- **HELIX** is the workflow and methodology layer built on that substrate.\n\nThe goal is to keep execution/storage/platform concerns in DDx while keeping\nworkflow semantics, autonomy behavior, and prompt strategy in HELIX.\n\nThis contract exists to prevent two common failure modes:\n1. HELIX re-implementing platform capabilities DDx already provides\n2. DDx absorbing HELIX-specific workflow behavior and becoming methodology-bound\n\n## DDx-Owned Substrate Responsibilities\n\nHELIX expects DDx to provide these platform capabilities.\n\n### 1. Graph primitives\n- Document graph indexing\n- `[[ID]]` body-link indexing in addition to frontmatter dependencies\n- Upstream/downstream traversal primitives\n- Reverse/dependent lookup primitives\n- Discovery of graph-authored execution documents\n\n### 2. Agent execution substrate\n- `ddx agent run` as the general harness interface\n- `ddx agent execute-bead \u003cbead-id\u003e [--from \u003crev\u003e] [--no-merge]` as the\n  canonical git-aware single-bead execution primitive\n- `ddx agent execute-loop [--once] [--poll-interval \u003cduration\u003e]` as the\n  canonical single-project queue-drain primitive that claims ready beads,\n  runs `execute-bead`, and closes merged work with evidence\n- Standard harness/model/effort/preset controls available through the DDx agent surface\n- Session capture, transcript capture, and runtime evidence capture\n\n### 3. Execution and metric substrate\n- Graph-authored execution documents as authoritative definitions\n- Immutable execution runs stored in the DDx execution substrate\n- Metric projection over execution runs\n- Required execution semantics sufficient to decide merge vs preserve\n- Metric ratchet evaluation sufficient to decide merge vs preserve\n\n### 4. Git-context execution mechanics\n- Dirty-tree checkpointing before managed execution\n- Isolated worktree execution\n- Preserved hidden refs for non-landed attempts\n- Rebase only to prepare fast-forward landing\n- Fast-forward landing when merge-eligible\n- Guaranteed worktree cleanup after landing or preservation\n\n### 5. Always-on runtime metrics and provenance\nFor every managed execute-bead attempt, including attempts launched by\n`ddx agent execute-loop`, DDx should capture runtime facts such as:\n- harness\n- model\n- session ID\n- elapsed duration\n- token usage\n- cost\n- base revision\n- result revision\n\n### 6. Repo-local DDx configuration surfaces\n- `.ddx/config.yaml`\n- installed DDx skills/bootstrap assets\n- preset/model resolution surfaces used by HELIX\n\n## HELIX-Owned Workflow / Methodology Responsibilities\n\nHELIX owns workflow behavior on top of the DDx substrate.\n\n### 1. Autonomy semantics\nHELIX defines what `low`, `medium`, and `high` autonomy mean behaviorally.\nDDx does not own these semantics.\n\n### 2. Workflow routing and supervision\nHELIX owns:\n- `helix input`\n- `helix run`\n- planning vs execution routing\n- supervisory stop/continue behavior\n- when to ask for human input\n- when to delegate a current project queue to `ddx agent execute-loop`\n- bead authoring rules for deterministic acceptance and success-measurement\n  criteria so DDx-managed execution can close merged work without manual\n  interpretation\n- bead topology policy for parent-child relationships and dependencies when\n  queue order matters (curation — ensures DDX's deterministic ordering is correct;\n  HELIX does not predict which bead DDX will select)\n\n### 3. Artifact-flow policy\nHELIX owns:\n- authority ordering across artifact layers\n- change propagation policy through the artifact stack\n- graph traversal policy beyond the primitive graph operations DDx exposes\n- search fallback policy when declared links are incomplete\n\n### 4. Conflict and escalation policy\nHELIX owns:\n- resolvable vs physics-level conflict classification\n- the vocabulary and meaning of those conflict classes\n- escalation behavior\n- follow-on bead creation\n- interpretation of preserved attempts for workflow continuation\n\nThe conflict taxonomy is HELIX-defined:\n- **Resolvable**: workflow may continue with escalation, assumptions, or follow-on beads\n- **Physics-level**: workflow must stop for human resolution because the governing intent is genuinely contradictory\n\nDDx may return execution outcomes such as merged or preserved, but it does not define HELIX conflict classes.\n\nHELIX interprets each bead as a governed workspace-state transformation:\n\n- bead `B` defines the intended transition from workspace state `W` to\n  successor workspace state `W'`\n- the execution run is the attempt and evidence record for realizing `B : W -\u003e W'`\n- the execution outcome describes how that attempt landed\n- the state delta is the realized material change between `W` and `W'`\n\n### 5. Prompt and workflow strategy\nHELIX owns:\n- prompt design\n- prompt engineering strategy\n- stage-authored behavior stance for planning, execution, review, alignment,\n  and related workflow stages\n- bead prompt structure\n- workflow wording and intervention policy\n- execution-document authoring conventions for HELIX artifacts (see [CONTRACT-002](CONTRACT-002-helix-execution-doc-conventions.md))\n\nHELIX does **not** expose stage personalities as a separate first-class\nworkflow configuration surface. The simpler contract is:\n\n- HELIX bakes stage stance into the governing action prompt, skill wording, or\n  execution-doc convention for that stage\n- DDx still owns harness/model execution and any concrete model resolution\n- if a stage needs a smarter, cheaper, slower, or faster lane, HELIX may ask\n  for tier or harness constraints, but it must not turn stage stance into\n  concrete model policy\n\nThe default HELIX stage stances are:\n\n| Stage family | Stance owned by HELIX | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Planning (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`, `triage`, `polish`) | exploratory, assumption-surfacing, artifact-authoring | widen context, expose ambiguity, prefer reversible shaping |\n| Managed execution (`build`, `measure`, execution-ready bead work) | contract-following, bounded, anti-feature-creep | implement only the governed slice and prove it deterministically |\n| Review (`review`, fresh-eyes passes) | adversarial, defect-seeking, risk-first | findings first; preserve merge/preserve evidence boundary |\n| Alignment (`align`) | top-down, conservative, drift-seeking | compare lower layers against higher-authority artifacts |\n| Supervisory/mechanical (`check`, `report`, queue steering) | concise, state-oriented, policy-applying | route work without inventing new product behavior |\n\nThese stances apply whether HELIX launches work through DDx-managed execution\nor through a direct non-managed prompt such as `ddx agent run` for planning,\nreview, or alignment. The stage selects the stance; DDx resolves the execution\nvehicle and concrete model policy.\n\n## Shared Integration Objects\n\nThese objects are shared across the boundary and should keep stable meanings.\n\n| Object | DDx role | HELIX role |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Bead** | tracker record + execution target | intended workspace-state transform (`B : W -\u003e W'`) |\n| **Workspace state** | execution substrate input/output surface | governed current/successor state under workflow interpretation |\n| **State delta** | realized material diff between workspace states | workflow-visible change to inspect during measure/report |\n| **Graph artifact** | indexed document node | governance/context layer |\n| **Execution doc** | discovered executable validation definition | authored validation contract |\n| **Execution run** | immutable evidence record | workflow input for measure/report/iteration |\n| **Metric** | structured runtime observation | workflow signal |\n| **Ratchet** | threshold/baseline evaluation | policy input for workflow decisions |\n| **Preserved attempt** | non-landed managed execution result | experiment/review/iteration input |\n\n## Workflow Handoff Points\n\n### HELIX -\u003e DDx\nHELIX decides:\n- what autonomy behavior should apply\n- what workflow prompt/context to use\n- what stage-authored stance should apply for the selected workflow step\n- when implementation/verification should be dispatched\n- whether to dispatch one bounded attempt with `ddx agent execute-bead` or\n  hand a single-project ready queue to `ddx agent execute-loop`\n\nHELIX then hands execution to DDx through managed agent/execution surfaces.\n\n### DDx -\u003e HELIX\nDDx returns evidence, not workflow policy:\n- execution outcome\n- queue-drain result summaries when `execute-loop` is used\n- required execution results\n- ratchet results\n- runtime metrics\n- landed vs preserved outcome\n- transcript/session/exec evidence locations\n\nMinimum workflow-visible outcome surface from `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"project_root\": \"/path\",\n  \"attempts\": 1,\n  \"successes\": 1,\n  \"failures\": 0,\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"bead_id\": \"hx-abc123\",       \"attempt_id\": \"...\",\n      \"harness\": \"codex\",           \"status\": \"success\",\n      \"detail\": \"...\",              \"session_id\": \"...\",\n      \"base_rev\": \"sha\",             \"result_rev\": \"sha\",\n      \"retry_after\": \"...\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n`results[].status` values:\n\n| Status | Meaning |\n|--------|---------|\n| `success` | Bead merged or preserved with success |\n| `no_changes` | Agent produced no tracked changes; worktree clean |\n| `execution_failed` | Agent or harness exit non-zero |\n| `land_conflict` | Attempt preserved: rebase failed or fast-forward not possible |\n| `post_run_check_failed` | Attempt preserved: post-run checks failed |\n| `structural_validation_failed` | Bead malformed or missing required fields |\n\nOutcome-to-status mapping (internal to DDx):\n- `outcome=merged` → `status=success`\n- `outcome=no-changes` → `status=no_changes`\n- `outcome=preserved`, reason in (rebase failed, ff-merge failed, ff-merge not possible) → `status=land_conflict`\n- `outcome=preserved`, reason=post-run checks failed → `status=post_run_check_failed`\n- `outcome=preserved`, other reason → `status=success`\n\nHELIX uses `results[].bead_id` for all post-cycle bookkeeping (not a pre-selected bead).\nHELIX uses `results[].result_rev` as the closing SHA candidate.\nHELIX uses `results[].retry_after` for blocked/suspended bead surfaced via `ddx bead blocked`.\n\nHELIX-authored execution beads must make success machine-auditable. In\npractice this means deterministic acceptance and success-measurement criteria:\nexact commands, named checks, concrete files or fields to inspect, and\nobservable end states. Vague success text such as \"works correctly\" is not a\nsufficient contract for `ddx agent execute-bead` or `ddx agent execute-loop`.\n\nWhen HELIX delegates queue draining to `ddx agent execute-loop`, DDx owns the\nclaim/execute/close mechanics for merged work in that loop. HELIX remains\nresponsible for deciding when loop delegation is appropriate and for\ninterpreting preserved, failed, or blocked outcomes.\n\nA preserved outcome is an execution result from a bounded DDx-managed attempt. It is not, by itself, a HELIX physics-level conflict; it hands control back to HELIX for workflow interpretation.\n\nHELIX then decides what to do next:\n- continue\n- escalate\n- ask for input\n- create follow-on beads\n- revise prompts/workflow wording\n\n## Anti-Drift Rules\n\n### DDx must not absorb\n- HELIX autonomy semantics\n- HELIX workflow routing logic\n- HELIX escalation policy\n- HELIX prompt engineering strategy\n- HELIX artifact decomposition policy\n\n### HELIX must not build in parallel\n- a second graph engine if DDx provides graph indexing/traversal\n- a second execution store\n- a second metrics/provenance store\n- a separate prompt-version registry\n- custom git execution mechanics that bypass the DDx managed execution flow\n- a second single-project queue-drain loop once `ddx agent execute-loop`\n  satisfies the required HELIX supervision contract\n\n## Queue Curation Policy\n\nHELIX owns queue curation. DDx owns bead selection and execution.\n\nHELIX does not predict which bead DDx will select. Instead, HELIX ensures the\nqueue is in the correct state so DDx's deterministic `ReadyExecution()` ordering\nproduces the intended sequence.\n\n| Queue mechanism | DDx behavior | HELIX responsibility |\n|---|---|---|\n| `dep-ids` | `ReadyExecution` waits for all deps to be `closed` | Set correctly on all beads; update when deps are added |\n| `parent` hierarchy | DDx respects parent/child but does not enforce child execution order | Epic execution is separate from child execution; children enter ready queue independently |\n| `execution-eligible` | `ReadyExecution` filters out `false`; defaults `true` if absent | Set `false` to temporarily suppress a bead without closing it |\n| `superseded-by` | `ReadyExecution` filters out superseded beads | Set when a bead is superseded; do not leave superseded beads open |\n| `execute-loop-retry-after` | `ReadyExecution` filters out beads on cooldown | DDx sets this on failed attempts; HELIX should not set it |\n\n### Epic-focus queue curation\n\nWhen HELIX enters epic-focus mode (stay on an epic until all children are done):\n\n1. HELIX sets `execution-eligible: false` on all non-child open beads via `ddx bead update --set execution-eligible=false`.\n2. DDx's `ReadyExecution` naturally picks only eligible children.\n3. When the epic closes or switches, HELIX removes the `execution-eligible` suppression from remaining beads.\n4. No DDX flag or special selection logic is required.\n\n### Queue-drift ownership\n\nDrift (superseded-by, parent change, spec-id change) is a **pre-execution** concern.\nHELIX detects drift at `helix check` time and prevents stale beads from entering\nthe ready queue. DDx executes what it is given; it does not reopen beads after\nclose-with-evidence.\n\nHELIX does not implement post-close reopen logic. If a bead was ready when DDx\npicked it, the execution result stands.\n\n## Queue-Injected Supervisory Beads\n\nReview and alignment are **regular beads**, not post-cycle hooks. HELIX injects them\ninto the queue and lets DDx execute them through execute-loop:\n\n| Bead type | Trigger | Acceptance |\n|---|---|---|\n| `review-finding` | Post-build or periodic | Agent finds 0 new drift violations |\n| `alignment-review` | Every N completed passes | No governance drift detected |\n\nWhen HELIX injects a review or alignment bead into the queue, DDx picks it up\nlike any other ready bead. The execution produces the same evidence bundle as\nimplementation beads, enabling performance analysis.\n\n`--no-auto-review` and `--no-auto-align` control whether HELIX injects these beads\n— they do not disable post-cycle hooks.\n\n## Performance Metadata and Optimization\n\nEvery execute-bead attempt produces:\n\n```\nbead_id → { new_state, performance_metadata }\n```\n\nPerformance metadata captured by DDx per attempt:\n- model, harness, provider\n- elapsed time\n- token usage (input, output, total)\n- cost\n- base revision, result revision\n- session ID, attempt ID\n\nHELIX uses this for:\n- **Prompt optimization**: did a simpler prompt produce the same result faster?\n- **Model routing**: would a faster/cheaper model have sufficed? Would a smarter model have avoided the failure?\n- **Process improvement**: why did this experiment fail? What assumption was wrong?\n\nHELIX experiments use this infrastructure:\n- Queue a batch of experiment beads with specific acceptance criteria\n- DDx runs them all in order via execute-loop\n- Each produces: new state + performance metadata\n- Keep the ones that improve state; failed experiments leave a detailed record\n  of what didn't work and why, enabling targeted improvement\n\nThis turns every bead execution into a measurable data point for continuous\nprocess optimization.\n\n## Post-Cycle HELIX Behavior\n\nAfter `ddx agent execute-loop --once` returns, HELIX applies post-cycle\nsupervisory policy to the bead(s) in `results[].bead_id`.\n\n| Behavior | Owner | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| Queue injection (review, alignment) | **HELIX** | Creates beads, DDx executes them |\n| Epic promotion (task → epic when children appear) | **HELIX** | Post-cycle tracker mutation |\n| Acceptance-failure filing | **HELIX** | Creates follow-on beads |\n| Cycle counting | **HELIX** | Increments on `status=success` |\n| Context refresh | **HELIX** | Every 5 cycles or on epic switch |\n| Closing SHA sync | **DDx** | Uses `results[].result_rev` |\n| Build gate (pre-merge check) | **DDx** | Runs before merge; reverts on failure |\n| Retry suppression | **DDx** | Sets `execute-loop-retry-after` on cooldown |\n| Orphan worktree recovery | **DDx** | Automatic after crashed runs |\n\nBehaviors **deleted** from HELIX wrapper:\n- HELIX retry/backoff logic (superseded by DDx `retry-after`)\n- HELIX blocker tracking and reporting (superseded by `ddx bead blocked`)\n- HELIX orphan tracker recovery (redundant with DDx worktree recovery)\n- `git checkout -- .` cleanup (DDx worktrees are isolated)\n- `safe_unclaim` after failed attempt (DDx handles claim lifecycle)\n- `HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE` env var (DDx never consumed it)\n\n## Validation Checklist\n\nThe boundary is healthy when all of the following are true:\n\n- [ ] HELIX uses DDx graph primitives instead of a parallel graph implementation for document indexing/traversal\n- [ ] HELIX uses DDx-managed bead execution instead of directly inventing its own execution/provenance substrate\n- [ ] HELIX parses `execute-loop --json` to find executed bead(s), applies post-cycle\n  bookkeeping to those bead IDs rather than to a pre-selected bead\n- [ ] HELIX does not pass `HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE` or any bead selector to DDx execute-loop\n- [ ] HELIX uses `execution-eligible` for epic-focus queue curation, not DDX flags\n- [ ] HELIX injects review and alignment as regular beads into the queue, not as post-cycle hooks\n- [ ] HELIX uses performance metadata from execute-bead results for prompt and model routing optimization\n- [ ] HELIX experiments use execute-loop to run batched experiment beads with performance tracking\n- [ ] HELIX does not implement retry/backoff or blocker tracking (DDx handles via `retry_after`)\n- [ ] HELIX does not implement post-close reopen logic (drift is pre-execution)\n- [ ] HELIX execution beads carry deterministic success criteria and\n  measurement hooks precise enough for DDx-managed close-with-evidence\n- [ ] DDx provides enough runtime evidence for HELIX to evaluate preserved attempts and landed runs\n- [ ] HELIX autonomy behavior is documented in HELIX, not DDx\n- [ ] DDx execution/metric/git semantics are documented in DDx, not redefined in HELIX\n- [ ] Preserved attempts, execution runs, and runtime metrics have one authoritative home in DDx\n- [ ] HELIX workflow docs describe how they consume DDx results rather than duplicating DDx implementation details\n\n## References\n\n- [Product Vision](../../00-discover/product-vision.md)\n- [FEAT-011: Slider Autonomy Control](../../01-frame/features/FEAT-011-slider-autonomy.md)\n- [ADR-001: HELIX Supervisory Control Model](../adr/ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md)\n- [API-001: HELIX Tracker Mutation Surface](API-001-helix-tracker-mutation.md)\n- [CONTRACT-002: HELIX Execution-Document Conventions](CONTRACT-002-helix-execution-doc-conventions.md)\n","tool_call_id":"451117445"},{"role":"assistant","content":"\n\n","tool_calls":[{"id":"950758123","name":"read","arguments":{"path":"./docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-slider-autonomy.md"}}]},{"role":"tool","content":"# FEAT-011: Slider Autonomy Control\n\n**Status**: Planning  \n**Priority**: P1  \n**Created**: 2026-04-07  \n**Related ADRs**: [ADR-001](../../02-design/adr/ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md) (supervisory control model)\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nCurrent HELIX has 15+ skills that mirror CLI commands. This creates cognitive load and doesn't match the actual user workflow, which is:\n\n1. **Two primary modes**: \"take design input\" vs \"work the queue\"\n2. **Human input is sparse**: \"design a CRM\", \"use postgres\", \"make logout blue\", \"here's a bug\"\n3. **Agent must maintain artifact stack coherence**: Vision → PRD → Specs → ADRs → Designs → Tests → Code\n\nThe current command-centric model doesn't support:\n- Natural language input interpretation\n- Cross-cutting concern enforcement (e.g., \"use postgres\" should check existing SQLite ADR)\n- Human-agentic continuum (sometimes human does work, sometimes agent runs away)\n- Non-blocking conflict escalation\n- **Automated artifact graph traversal**\n\n## Requirements\n\n### FR-01: Artifact Graph Metadata (Based on Existing Hierarchy)\nEach artifact shall declare its connections using the existing HELIX cross-reference pattern:\n\n```markdown\n# TD-036-list-mcp-servers.md\n**User Story**: [[US-036-list-mcp-servers]]\n**Parent Feature**: [[FEAT-001-mcp-server-management]]\n**Solution Design**: [[SD-001-mcp-management]]\n```\n\nGraph metadata includes:\n- **Upstream dependencies**: Higher-authority artifacts (PRD, FEAT, SD) that govern this one\n- **Downstream dependents**: Lower-authority artifacts (TD → TP → tests → code) derived from this one\n- **Peer relationships**: Same-level artifacts (US-036 ↔ US-037 in same feature)\n\n**Storage**: Cross-references embedded in artifact frontmatter/body using `[[ID]]` notation. No separate registry needed initially.\n\n### FR-02: Impact Detection via Search + Declared Links\nThe system shall identify impacted artifacts through two-phase detection:\n\n1. **Declared links first**: Follow existing `[[ID]]` cross-references from changed artifact\n2. **Search-based fallback**: Use `rg` to find term matches (e.g., \"Postgres\" → ADRs about database choice)\n\n**Precedence**: Declared links take precedence over search results to avoid false positives. Search only supplements when declared links are incomplete.\n\n### FR-03: Graph Traversal and Change Flow\nOnce impacted artifacts are identified, the system shall:\n\n1. **Trace the graph**: Follow upstream/downstream connections from impact point\n2. **Order by authority**: Process in canonical order (Vision → PRD → FEAT → US → SD → TD → TP → Tests → Code)\n   - Note: ADRs can appear at multiple levels; they govern both specs AND designs\n3. **Flow changes**: Propagate modifications through each layer as atomic diffs\n4. **Create downstream work**: Generate beads for artifacts that need updating\n\n**Cycle handling**: Traversal uses visited set to prevent infinite loops on peer relationships.\n\n### FR-04: Physics-Level vs Resolvable Conflict Detection\nThe system shall distinguish between:\n\n| Type | Definition | Example | Action |\n|------|-----------|---------|--------|\n| **Resolvable** | Different approaches to same goal | Postgres vs SQLite for database | Escalate via bead, continue with documented assumption |\n| **Physics-level** | True contradictions that cannot coexist | \"must be real-time\" + \"batch processing only\" | Block execution, require human resolution |\n\nHigh autonomy mode proceeds through resolvable conflicts; blocks only on physics-level.\n\nDDx preserve outcomes from bounded execution attempts (for example failed required executions or ratchet regressions) are **not** physics-level conflicts. They terminate the current DDx-managed attempt and hand control back to HELIX for escalation, follow-on beads, or user input.\n\n### FR-05: Autonomy Slider Controls Flow Behavior\nThe system shall support configurable autonomy level (per session or project):\n\n| Level | Behavior | Use Case |\n|-------|----------|----------|\n| **Low** | Ask before each graph step and before creating each downstream artifact; do **not** make changes until the user approves | Learning mode, critical systems |\n| **Medium** | Traverse graph autonomously, ask only on conflicts/ambiguity | Default for most work |\n| **High** | Continue end-to-end autonomously; escalate resolvable conflicts as non-blocking beads; only physics-level conflicts stop the supervisory workflow, while DDx preserve outcomes end the current bounded attempt and hand control back to HELIX | Trusted contexts, rapid iteration |\n\n### FR-06: Minimal Execution Surface (Backward Compatible)\nExecution entrypoints reduce to two core capabilities while maintaining backward compatibility:\n\n1. **Input command**: `helix input \"\u003cnatural language\u003e\"` - accepts sparse input, triggers graph traversal\n2. **Queue-drain command**: `ddx agent execute-loop` - drains execution-ready beads using DDx-managed claim, execute-bead, land/preserve, and close-with-evidence semantics\n\n`helix run` remains as a compatibility wrapper and operator convenience surface\nwhile DDx loop parity is validated. If `execute-loop` plus HELIX skills reach\nthe required parity, HELIX-owned execution wrappers (`helix run`, and\npotentially `helix build`) become deprecation candidates while input,\nplanning, review, and spec-shaping surfaces remain HELIX-owned.\n\nPlanning actions such as alignment do not need their own permanent execution\nsubstrate. If `helix align` remains as a skill or CLI surface, it must behave\nas a thin entrypoint that creates or claims the governing alignment bead and\nthen invokes the stored alignment prompt.\n\n### FR-07: Bead-Centric Coordination\nAll work flows through beads (non-blocking for resolvable conflicts):\n\n- User input → [Bead: Request]\n- Agent refines → [Children: Questions/Decisions]\n- Conflicts → [Bead: Escalation - non-blocking, label `kind:escalation`]\n- Gaps → [Bead: Gap - speculative, label `kind:speculative`]\n\nWorkers execute from the bead queue without blocking on resolvable conflicts. Physics-level conflicts block only the affected worker path.\n\n### FR-08: Verification Loop and Traceability\nThe system shall verify that artifact flow produces **functional, measurable results**:\n\n#### Traceability Model (Based on Existing Pattern)\n```\nFEAT-001 → SD-001 → US-036 → TD-036 → TP-036 → tests → code\n         ↓\n         US-037 → TD-037 → TP-037 → tests → code\n```\n\nEach layer references upstream via `[[ID]]` notation. Tests reference spec IDs in comments or metadata.\n\n#### Failure Triage Algorithm\nWhen verification fails:\n1. **Classify failure type**:\n   - Test bug (test wrong) → create bead for test fix\n   - Implementation bug (code wrong) → create bead for code fix  \n   - Spec conflict (spec contradicts upstream) → trace back to spec, create escalation bead\n2. **Trace back through graph** using `[[ID]]` references: failing test → TP → TD → US/FEAT → PRD\n3. **Identify adjustment point**: nearest upstream artifact that directly specifies the failing behavior\n4. **Create follow-on beads** for resolution\n\n#### Metrics and Non-Functional Requirements\nFor non-testable constraints (latency, security, compliance):\n- Define named metrics in artifacts (e.g., \"P95 \u003c 100ms\" in PRD)\n- Automated checks collect metrics continuously\n- Threshold violations trigger reflow via beads\n\n## DDx Substrate vs HELIX Workflow Ownership\n\nThis section summarizes the canonical boundary defined in [`CONTRACT-001`](../../02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-ddx-helix-boundary.md). If this feature text and the contract diverge, the contract is authoritative.\n\n**DDx owns the substrate**:\n- graph primitives (`[[ID]]` indexing, upstream/downstream traversal, reverse lookup)\n- graph-discovered execution docs and required validation execution\n- managed bead execution via `ddx agent execute-bead \u003cbead-id\u003e [--from \u003crev\u003e] [--no-merge]`\n- single-project queue draining via `ddx agent execute-loop`\n- runtime evidence capture, execution runs, metric projection, ratchet evaluation, and merge/preserve/close mechanics\n\n**HELIX owns the workflow semantics**:\n- autonomy behavior (`low` / `medium` / `high`)\n- authority ordering and artifact-flow policy\n- conflict classification and escalation behavior\n- workflow routing (`helix input`, when to delegate a queue to `execute-loop`, follow-on bead creation)\n- deterministic bead acceptance and success-measurement authoring\n- prompt design and prompt-engineering strategy\n- stage-authored behavior stance for planning, execution, review, alignment, and supervisory steps\n\nHELIX does not need a separate user-facing \"personality profile\" configuration\nsurface to preserve this ownership. The intended contract is simpler:\n\n- stage stance lives in HELIX action prompts, skill wording, and execution-doc conventions\n- DDx still owns harness/model execution plus concrete model resolution\n- HELIX may request stage-appropriate tier or harness constraints, but it must\n  not turn stage stance into duplicated model-version policy\n\nDefault stage families:\n\n- planning (`input`, `frame`, `design`, `evolve`, `triage`, `polish`): exploratory, ambiguity-surfacing, artifact-authoring\n- managed execution (`build`, `measure`): bounded, contract-following, anti-feature-creep\n- review: adversarial and risk-first\n- alignment: top-down and drift-seeking\n- supervisory (`check`, `report`): concise, state-oriented, policy-applying\n\n**Handoff contract**: HELIX decides scope, autonomy behavior, queue policy, and\nworkflow context; DDx executes either one bounded bead (`execute-bead`) or the\nproject queue (`execute-loop`) and returns runtime evidence plus merge/preserve\noutcomes; HELIX interprets preserved, failed, or blocked results to continue,\nescalate, or ask for input. Direct `ddx agent run` remains appropriate for\nplanning, review, alignment, and other non-managed prompts that should not\nclaim and close beads automatically; those direct prompts should reuse the same\nHELIX-authored stage stance rather than inventing a separate personality lane.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n### AC-01: Artifact Graph Metadata\n- [ ] Existing `[[ID]]` cross-reference pattern works for graph traversal\n- [ ] Traversal can follow declared connections automatically (tested with fixture artifacts)\n- [ ] Search-based impact detection supplements declared relationships when links incomplete\n\n### AC-02: Impact Detection and Traversal  \n- [ ] Given sample artifact graph, change in PRD produces exactly expected downstream beads\n- [ ] Graph traversal follows canonical authority order with ADR exceptions handled correctly\n- [ ] Cycle detection prevents infinite loops on peer relationships\n\n### AC-03: Conflict Classification\n- [ ] System distinguishes resolvable vs physics-level conflicts (test cases defined)\n- [ ] Resolvable conflicts create escalation beads and continue with documented assumptions\n- [ ] Physics-level conflicts block execution and require human resolution\n\n### AC-04: Autonomy Slider Behavior\n- [ ] Low autonomy: asks before each graph step astified\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eworkflows/phases/02-design/GATE.yaml\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview the deleted artifact type 'security-architecture'. Determine whether its original intent still exists in current HELIX, whether that intent is already covered by another canonical artifact or tracker primitive, and whether 'security-architecture' should be restored, superseded, or retired. If restoration is warranted, define the minimum acceptable prompt and template bar for 'security-architecture' so the artifact is not reintroduced as another thin stub.\n\nIntent to validate: Security architecture patterns, trust boundaries, controls, and design-level security decisions that shape implementation and testing.","id":"helix-083bb26e","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:design","kind:docs","area:artifacts"],"owner":"erik","parent":"helix-fef22846","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"workflows/phases/02-design/GATE.yaml","status":"closed","title":"Review deleted artifact type: security-architecture","updated_at":"2026-04-09T22:00:45.757288489Z"}

A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. str: path = resolve_artifact_path(root, ref) if path is None or not path.exists(): return \"\" markdown = read_text(path) adr_id = extract_doc_id(markdown) or strip_markdown(ref) title_match = re.search(r\"^#\\s+(.*)$\", markdown, re.MULTILINE) title = strip_markdown(title_match.group(1)) if title_match else adr_id title = re.sub(rf\"^{re.escape(adr_id)}:\\s*\", \"\", title) decision = choose_best_clause( iter_markdown_clauses(extract_section(markdown, \"Decision\")), \"decision selected approach\", ) rationale = extract_adr_rationale(markdown) summary = f\"{adr_id} {title}\" if decision: summary += f\" — {decision}\" if rationale: summary += f\" Why: {rationale}\" return summary\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:340\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new secondary ADR discovery path is dead code. `secondary_adr_matches()` returns bare filenames like `ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md`, but `build_adrs()` passes those strings to `summarize_adr()`, which calls `resolve_artifact_path()`. That resolver only handles direct repo-relative paths, extracted document IDs, or filename stems, so a bare filename with `.md` never resolves. In the live repo, `secondary_adr_matches()` returns both ADR filenames for a workflow bead, while `build_adrs()` still returns `[]`. That means the FEAT-006 fallback path for ADRs not wired into concerns silently never contributes an `\u003cadrs\u003e` section.\nSuggested fix: Make secondary matches return repo-relative paths or ADR document IDs, or teach `resolve_artifact_path()` to resolve bare ADR filenames. Add a test that exercises the secondary-match path end to end so the fallback cannot regress Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \\ddx bead ready --json\\, \\ddx bead show \u0026lt;id\u0026gt;\\, \\ddx bead update \u0026lt;id\u0026gt; --claim\\, \\ddx bead close \u0026lt;id\u0026gt;\\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. .ddx/beads.jsonl:40:{"acceptance":"HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T01:12:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2780439","closing_commit_sha":"c7901bbd63076a17c67d26fca1ff8080595fd6a8","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:58:27.330158522Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eTheme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eworkflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nHELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.\n\nRequest: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.\n\nRequirements from this discussion:\n1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.\n2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.\n3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.\n4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.\n5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.\n6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.\n7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.\n8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.","events_attachment":"helix-1940a77b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev":"7f97067b3541319cac3ee3889691912844d95ae3","execute-loop-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-14T18:01:00Z","model":"mlx-community/Qwen3.6-27B-8bit","provider":"grendel-rapid-mlx","reason":"provider_connectivity"}],"execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: provider error: openai: Post \"http://grendel:8000/v1/chat/completions\": dial tcp 100.97.179.68:8000: i/o timeout","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-05-14T18:16:01Z","id":"helix-1940a77b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:design","kind:spec","area:docs","area:workflow"],"notes":"Sub-agent ae60... committed scope changes (selection rules, non-HTTP suitability, CONTRACT-* canonical with API-* allowed HTTP subtype). .ddx/beads.jsonl:48:{"acceptance":"All workflow-bundle and artifact-doc findings for this area are classified with evidence and reflected in the durable report","created_at":"2026-04-11T06:40:08.56743796Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003ehelix.implementation-plan — This build plan defines the current HELIX execution landscape: which features are implemented, which have remaining work, what the governing artifacts are, and what ordering constraints keep implementation aligned with the authority stack.\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview workflow bundle docs, templates, and implementation-plan snapshots for tracker-path or artifact-contract drift against the repo-wide HELIX contract.","id":"helix-1e5e7ec9","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:review","kind:review","area:artifacts","area:docs","area:workflow"],"parent":"helix-fd9c1ef7","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"helix.implementation-plan","status":"closed","title":"Alignment review: repo - workflow bundle and artifact docs","updated_at":"2026-04-11T06:48:23.326692127Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:66:{"acceptance":"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads with scope-appropriate area labels; AGENTS.md updated if needed","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T13:18:38Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2036620","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:18:33.06730359Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003e2d2715d77ca54217d9d22312c03a4303ddf513a6\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nFresh-eyes review of last implementation commit.\nReview target: last-commit .ddx/beads.jsonl:69:{"acceptance":"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads with scope-appropriate area labels; AGENTS.md updated if needed","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T11:16:27Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"355954","closing_commit_sha":"607e6be1c42117a07a961d5e254e5c05eaeb7af3","created_at":"2026-04-11T11:16:27.076989482Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003ehelix-71aab0f0\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nFresh-eyes review of implementation commit 607e6be1c42117a07a961d5e254e5c05eaeb7af3 after tracker-only close commit fa4c0a0.\\nReview target: commit:607e6be1c42117a07a961d5e254e5c05eaeb7af3 (requested scope: last-commit).\\nReviewed issue: helix-71aab0f0.","id":"helix-2b0053d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:review","kind:planning","action:review","area:demo","area:docs","area:site","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"\u003cmeasure-results\u003e\n  \u003ctimestamp\u003e$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\u003c/timestamp\u003e\n  \u003cstatus\u003eCLEAN\u003c/status\u003e\n  \u003cfindings total='0' filed='0' critical='0' high='0' medium='0' low='0'/\u003e\n  \u003cagents-md-updated\u003eYES\u003c/agents-md-updated\u003e\n  \u003clearnings-filed\u003e0\u003c/learnings-filed\u003e\n\u003c/measure-results\u003e","owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"helix-71aab0f0","status":"closed","title":"review: last-commit 607e6be Pages demo recording regression coverage","updated_at":"2026-04-11T11:18:54.119397905Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:94:{"acceptance":"The deterministic deploy validation lane fails if workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md, workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md, or website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md omits one of deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, runbook, or release-notes or reintroduces stale three-artifact wording, passes when those surfaces match the canonical deploy contract, and git diff --check passes","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T12:45:05Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2292557","closing_commit_sha":"cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c","created_at":"2026-04-11T08:02:58.184221541Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003etests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh — repo_root=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")/..\" \u0026amp;\u0026amp; pwd)\" validator=\"$repo_root/scripts/validate_deploy_artifact_graph.py\" artifacts_dir=\"$repo_root/workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts\"\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh:1\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 253d14f4611502d1986a2bd5699c3329f7c17c19 repairs stale wording in `workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md`, but the checked-in validation surface still only verifies the deploy artifact dependency graph. `tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh` does not read `workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md`, `workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md`, or `website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md`, so the narrative four-artifact deploy contract can drift again without any deterministic failure. The previous stale three-artifact wording reached the repo because this contract edge is still enforced only by ad hoc review and commit-message grep, not by a checked-in validator.\nSuggested fix: Extend the deterministic deploy validation lane so it asserts the canonical four-artifact contract across the operator and shipped documentation surfaces that define it, at minimum the deploy README, deploy enforcer, and glossary artifacts page.","id":"helix-39fc1526","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:site","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"\u003cmeasure-results\u003e\n  \u003ctimestamp\u003e2026-04-11T12:47:47Z\u003c/timestamp\u003e\n  \u003cstatus\u003ePASS\u003c/status\u003e\n  \u003cacceptance\u003e\n    \u003ccriterion name='deploy-doc-contract' status='pass' evidence='scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py now enforces the canonical four-artifact wording across the deploy README, deploy enforcer, and glossary surfaces.'/\u003e\n    \u003ccriterion name='deterministic-regression-fixture' status='pass' evidence='tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh now includes a three-artifact regression fixture and fails it deterministically.'/\u003e\n    \u003ccriterion name='repo-hygiene' status='pass' evidence='git diff --check passed after the validator changes.'/\u003e\n  \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n  \u003cgates\u003e\n    \u003cgate concern='workflow/testing' command='bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh' status='pass'/\u003e\n    \u003cgate concern='repo-hygiene' command='git diff --check' status='pass'/\u003e\n    \u003cgate concern='pre-push' command='lefthook run pre-commit' status='pass'/\u003e\n  \u003c/gates\u003e\n\u003c/measure-results\u003e","owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh","status":"closed","title":"drift: add deterministic coverage for deploy contract docs","updated_at":"2026-04-11T15:43:39.442551385Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:95:{"acceptance":"Review-filed finding beads include at least one scope-appropriate area:* label derived from the reviewed scope or governing bead, and the fresh-eyes review workflow documentation reflects that requirement deterministically.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T19:44:33Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"213139","created_at":"2026-04-10T11:42:19.513617383Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eTheme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eworkflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md — Review-filed finding beads include at least one scope-appropriate area:* label derived from the reviewed scope or governing bead, and the fresh-eyes review workflow documentation reflects that requirement deterministically.\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: workflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md:188\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The fresh-eyes review filing pattern creates follow-on build beads with only helix, phase:build, and review-finding labels. .ddx/beads.jsonl:101:{"acceptance":"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads with scope-appropriate area labels; AGENTS.md updated if needed","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T12:32:00Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"784309","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:31:54.459387024Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eb8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nFresh-eyes review of commit b8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96.\\nReview target: commit:b8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96\\nReviewed execution bead: helix-d056ee1d","id":"helix-3cb92cbd","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:review","kind:planning","action:review","area:artifacts","area:cli","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"\u003cmeasure-results\u003e\n  \u003ctimestamp\u003e2026-04-11T12:35:31Z\u003c/timestamp\u003e\n  \u003cstatus\u003eISSUES_FOUND\u003c/status\u003e\n  \u003cfindings total='1' filed='1' critical='0' high='0' medium='1' low='0'/\u003e\n  \u003cagents-md-updated\u003eNO\u003c/agents-md-updated\u003e\n  \u003clearnings-filed\u003e0\u003c/learnings-filed\u003e\n\u003c/measure-results\u003e","owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"b8d49ca858f5d45f36f2b6fc95689877d69fce96","status":"closed","title":"review: commit b8d49ca artifact-area align root follow-up","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:35:31.985353068Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:116:{"acceptance":"helix run only applies drift handling, epic bookkeeping, acceptance-failure attribution, and review to the bead DDx actually executed, and bash tests/helix-cli.sh includes a regression case that passes without relying on HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE.","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:08:06.889269426Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"helix-4243dd31","depends_on_id":"helix-f3062aa2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-11T08:08:52Z"}],"description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — check_prompt() { local scope=\"${1:-repo}\" cat \u0026lt;\u0026lt;EOF Execute the following HELIX check action. $(inline_action \"${helix_docs_root}/actions/check.md\") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \\ddx bead ready --json\\, \\ddx bead list --json\\, \\ddx bead blocked --json\\. Review scope: ${scope}. CRITICAL: Your output MUST contain the line \"NEXT_ACTION: CODE\" where CODE is one of BUILD, DESIGN, POLISH, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, or STOP. This line must appear on its own, not inside a code block or backticks. Without this line the automation cannot continue. Output this line FIRST, before any other analysis. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/helix:1087\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: high\nDescription: run_loop still computes a specific selected_issue and then applies queue-drift checks, epic-focus bookkeeping, acceptance-failure attribution, and post-cycle review to that bead, but run_execute_loop_once delegates with ddx agent execute-loop --once and does not pass any supported selector. The DDx contract only says execute-loop claims the next ready bead, so HELIX can no longer assume DDx executed the same bead that local scheduling selected. If DDx drains a different ready item, HELIX will mark the wrong bead as failed, blocked, reviewed, or promoted. The test shim in tests/helix-cli.sh currently hides this mismatch by reading the private HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE environment variable, which is not part of the documented DDx surface.\nSuggested fix: preserve the HELIX-selected bead across the managed path, or consume the actual bead ID/result returned by DDx before any post-cycle bookkeeping. Add a deterministic harness case where DDx drains a different ready bead unless HELIX uses a supported selector/result surface.","id":"helix-4243dd31","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:cli","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"Cancelled during queue cleanup on 2026-05-15: superseded by the runtime-neutral HELIX scope in helix-f9207605 / helix.prd. DDx execution-wrapper work is no longer product-critical HELIX work; retain only if re-filed later as explicit DDx adapter maintenance.","parent":"helix-13cfe23f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"scripts/helix","status":"cancelled","superseded-by":"helix-e08a36d4","title":"integration: run loop delegates execute-loop without preserving HELIX-selected issue","updated_at":"2026-05-15T02:02:47.896754386Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:122:{"acceptance":"A stale in_progress align governing bead with a dead claiming PID is reclaimed and reused by helix align repo; a live in_progress bead owned by another actor still refuses duplicate dispatch; deterministic CLI coverage exercises both paths.","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T11:43:20Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3389734","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:59:05.135667303Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — Commands: run Loop over ready HELIX work, then check whether to continue doctor Check installation health; --fix auto-repairs plugin symlinks and skills start Launch helix run as a background process (PID file written) stop Kill the background helix run cleanly input Accept sparse intent and shape governed HELIX work frame Create or refine product vision, PRD, and feature specs build Run one bounded implementation pass check Run the bounded HELIX queue-health check align Run reconcile-alignment for a scope (default: repo) backfill Run documentation backfill for a scope (default: repo) design Create a comprehensive design document through iterative refinement polish Refine issues before implementation (dedup, coverage, criteria) next Show recommended next issue review Fresh-eyes review of recent work experiment Run one experiment iteration for metric-driven optimization evolve Thread a new requirement through the artifact stack commit Commit with HELIX format, run build gate, push, close issue triage Create a bead via ddx bead create\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/helix:817\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new align guard rejects every matching `status: in_progress` governing bead without checking whether the claim is stale. In a harness repro that seeds an `align: repo` bead claimed by dead PID `99999`, `helix align repo` now exits with `alignment already active for repo under owner helix (hx-align-stale); refusing duplicate dispatch` instead of reclaiming the stale governing bead and continuing. That leaves the reviewed issue's intended reclaim behavior unresolved for standalone `helix align` runs and any path that reaches `run_alignment()` without the run-loop orphan-recovery prepass.\nSuggested fix: Before failing duplicate dispatch, detect whether the in-progress align bead is orphaned (dead or absent claiming PID / beyond the reclaim threshold) and reclaim it through the existing tracker recovery path; keep the hard failure only for live active owners. Add a deterministic regression that covers stale-bead reclaim in addition to the live-owner rejection path.","id":"helix-43d339d5","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:cli","area:testing","area:workflow"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"scripts/helix","status":"closed","title":"integration: stale in-progress align bead blocks reclaim","updated_at":"2026-04-11T11:56:24.913856135Z"}

Product vision and FEAT-011 link to the contract doc as the source of truth for the boundary.","claimed-at":"2026-04-08T16:37:49Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"452607","created_at":"2026-04-08T16:28:56.041424234Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eProduct Vision; FEAT-011 Slider Autonomy; ADR-001 Supervisory Control Model\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003cfocus\u003eCreate one explicit HELIX contract document that states what DDx provides as substrate and what HELIX owns above it.\u003c/focus\u003e\n\u003cconstraints\u003eDo not duplicate DDx implementation detail. .ddx/beads.jsonl:129:{"acceptance":"If the helper remains the documented path, running it on a bead with matched ADR refs and a spec-id produces \u003cadrs\u003e and a governing clause extracted from the referenced artifact rather than only spec-id plus acceptance text. Otherwise, the workflow docs clearly scope the helper as partial and keep full digest assembly requirements elsewhere.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T12:52:38Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2561930","closing_commit_sha":"51abdcccf8d0764464ff76fc1266712acd3c5a43","created_at":"2026-04-10T12:38:34.734611867Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:205\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The refreshed workflow docs now tell triage, evolve, polish, and context-digest assembly to use the repo helper when it exists, but the helper does not implement the required ADR and governing-spec phases of the FEAT-006 assembly algorithm. `build_digest()` never resolves ADR references at all, and `build_governing()` reduces governing context to `spec-id` plus the first acceptance clause instead of loading the referenced artifact and extracting the governing requirement. The live digest on helix-7b3c6980 shows the result: no \u003cadrs\u003e block and only `FEAT-006 — Open beads in scope...` as governing context. That leaves future beads with materially less authority than the workflow contract promises while the docs advertise the helper as the canonical deterministic path.\nSuggested fix: Either extend the helper to resolve ADR references and governing clauses from the referenced spec files, or narrow the workflow docs so the helper is explicitly limited to label refresh/basic digest normalization until it implements the full FEAT-006 assembly contract.","id":"helix-46959498","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:workflow","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"scripts/refresh_context_digests.py","status":"closed","title":"drift: make digest helper honor ADR and governing-spec assembly requirements","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:57:23.389227468Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:134:{"acceptance":"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads with scope-appropriate area labels; AGENTS.md updated if needed","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T05:20:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3072254","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:20:20.907752832Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision.
