.ddx/beads.jsonl:181:{"acceptance":"`bash scripts/helix help` includes `helix input`; the help text documents the DDx-managed execution path and compatibility status of `helix run` / `helix build`; `bash tests/helix-cli.sh` passes.","created_at":"2026-04-11T02:25:07.401704176Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — review_prompt() { local scope=\"${1:-last-commit}\" local in_loop=\"${2:-0}\" cat \u0026lt;\u0026lt;EOF Execute the following HELIX fresh-eyes review action. $(inline_action \"${helix_docs_root}/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md\") Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \\ddx bead\\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${in_loop:+This review is running inside the automated helix run loop after an implementation cycle.} IMPORTANT: File each actionable finding (critical, high, medium severity) as a tracker issue with label \\review-finding\\ so findings are durable and queryable via \\ddx bead list --label review-finding\\. See the \"Filing Findings as Tracker Issues\" section in the action for the exact command pattern. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/helix:246\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The reviewed commit makes `helix input` the preferred first-class intake surface in FEAT-002, FEAT-011, workflows/EXECUTION.md, and workflows/REFERENCE.md, but the shipped `helix help` output still omits `helix input` entirely and presents `helix run` as the leading surface. Operators following the live CLI help cannot discover the new contract, so the docs-to-CLI boundary remains inconsistent after commit 466924b.\nSuggested fix: Add `helix input` to the usage/command help text with its autonomy flag shape, and update the help wording so `helix run` / `helix build` are clearly compatibility wrappers relative to the DDx-managed path. Add deterministic help-output coverage to prevent regression.","id":"helix-6132f8fa","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:cli","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"Verified during alignment pass helix-93e11c09: bash scripts/helix help now includes helix input and documents DDx-managed execution guidance for helix run / helix build; bash tests/helix-cli.sh passed 126 tests on 2026-04-10.","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"scripts/helix","status":"closed","title":"drift: expose helix input in live CLI help","updated_at":"2026-04-11T03:30:53.628576282Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:199:{"acceptance":"A freshly created align governing bead carries deterministic area:* labels and a context digest that includes the matched project concerns/practices for the reviewed scope before the stored alignment prompt begins","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T06:01:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3193390","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:58:33.163223998Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope=\"${1:-repo}\" governing_bead=\"${2:-}\" governing_mode=\"${3:-}\" local governing_note=\"Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding.\" if [[ -n \"$governing_bead\" ]]; then case \"$governing_mode\" in claimed) governing_note=\"Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially).\" ;; reused) governing_note=\"Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead).\" ;; created) governing_note=\"Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead).\" ;; esac fi cat \u0026lt;\u0026lt;EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action \"${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md\") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \\ddx bead\\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/helix:819\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: ensure_alignment_bead creates a new governing align bead with only helix,phase:review,kind:planning,action:align plus a generic description. .ddx/beads.jsonl:221:{"acceptance":"A checked-in deterministic repo test reproduces the Pages Record demos flow with a failing wrapped command and proves no cast is copied; the same test proves a passing wrapped command still copies a cast; .github/workflows/test.yml runs that test in the standard validation lane.","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T11:08:34Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3658920","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:22:24.173768407Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003e.github/workflows/test.yml — run: bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .github/workflows/test.yml:25\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 15f6198 fixes the Pages recording loop in .github/workflows/pages.yml, and local repro confirms the new status-file check blocks copying failed demo casts. But the repository's standard validation lane still never exercises that failure path: .github/workflows/test.yml only runs bash tests/helix-cli.sh, bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh, bash tests/validate-state-rules.sh, bash tests/validate-skills.sh, and bash tests/test-install.sh, and rg confirms none of those suites reference pages.yml, Record demos, or the new status-file contract. That leaves the fix protected only by one-off local repro notes in the commit message, which violates the active demo-asciinema and e2e-playwright expectation that user-facing demo/site workflows have deterministic regression checks rather than manual-only verification.\nSuggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that simulates the Pages Record demos shell flow for both failing and passing wrapped demo commands, asserts the failing case exits before cast copy, wire that test into the normal repo validation lane, and keep the acceptance aligned with A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — Options: --agent Agent harness to use: codex, claude, ddx-agent, embedded, or any harness from \\ddx agent list\\ (default: ${agent}) --claude Shorthand for --agent claude --codex Shorthand for --agent codex --ddx-agent Shorthand for --agent ddx-agent --embedded Shorthand for --agent embedded --forge Deprecated alias for the embedded DDx agent harness --model Explicit model override to pass through to DDx / the harness --effort Reasoning effort: low, medium, high, max (defaults vary by action) --review-every Run alignment every N completed implementation cycles --max-cycles Stop after N completed implementation cycles --no-auto-align Do not auto-run alignment when check returns ALIGN --no-auto-review Do not run fresh-eyes review after each implementation --review-threshold N Skip review for changes below N lines (default: 100) --review-agent Use a different agent for reviews (default: adversarial alternate) --dry-run Print the agent command instead of executing it --quiet, -q Suppress progress output (command echo, prompt sizes) --summary, -s Concise output with log-file line pointers (implies --quiet)\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/helix:1155\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: high\nDescription: The new generic `HELIX_MODEL` / `--model` surface is forwarded unconditionally to DDx-managed execution in `run_execute_bead` and `run_execute_loop_once` after `managed_build_agent` may switch the harness to the auto-selected alternate agent. In the default codex-primary / claude-alternate setup, `env HELIX_AGENT=codex HELIX_ALT_AGENT=claude HELIX_MODEL=gpt-5.4 bash scripts/helix build --dry-run ...` now emits `--harness claude --model gpt-5.4`, and `helix run --dry-run` emits the same cross-harness pair. That recreates exactly the concrete-model-policy coupling FEAT-011 / CONTRACT-001 / TD-011 were trying to remove and can make the new public override surface fail as soon as adversarial rotation is active.\nSuggested fix: Only pass an explicit model override to DDx-managed execution when the execution harness matches the requested primary harness, or otherwise disable/override adversarial rotation when `--model` is supplied. .ddx/beads.jsonl:237:{"acceptance":"scripts/validate_context_digests.py rejects generic HELIX beads that only use the omission-rationale prefix without explicit workflow authorization, still accepts beads from explicitly authorized omission paths, tests/validate-context-digests.sh covers both cases deterministically, and git diff --check passes.","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T11:21:29Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"924510","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:41:36.494375532Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003escripts/validate_context_digests.py — def main() -\u0026gt; int: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser( description=( \"Validate that open HELIX beads carry a context digest or an \" \"explicit omission rationale.\" ) ) parser.add_argument( \"--tracker\", default=\".ddx/beads.jsonl\", help=\"Tracker JSONL path (default: .ddx/beads.jsonl).\", ) parser.add_argument( \"--status\", action=\"append\", default=[], help=\"Status to validate (repeatable, default: open).\", ) args = parser.parse_args()\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:24\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new validator accepts any open HELIX bead whose description starts with `Explicit omission rationale:`, even though the FEAT-006 contract only permits that form when a governing workflow explicitly allows digest omission. A temp tracker containing a generic open build bead with `description=\"Explicit omission rationale: skipped for convenience\"` now exits 0 under `python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker \u003ctempfile\u003e`, and a repo-wide search finds no workflow/action that currently grants that exception. This turns the validator into a bypass: a bead can silently dodge the digest requirement by writing the magic prefix, so missing-digest regressions can pass the gate instead of being surfaced.\nSuggested fix: Make omission-rationale acceptance conditional on explicit workflow authorization that the validator can verify deterministically (for example a dedicated label/field set by the allowed creation paths), and add a negative fixture proving a generic HELIX bead with the prefix still fails validation.","id":"helix-7abd2548","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:artifacts","area:docs","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"\u003cmeasure-results\u003e\n  \u003ctimestamp\u003e2026-04-11T11:24:52Z\u003c/timestamp\u003e\n  \u003cstatus\u003ePASS\u003c/status\u003e\n  \u003cacceptance\u003e\n    \u003ccriterion name='authorized-omission-required' .ddx/beads.jsonl:270:{"acceptance":"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads with scope-appropriate area labels; AGENTS.md updated if needed","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T05:57:29Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3189167","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:57:21.31815475Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003ehelix-bec526ad\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nFresh-eyes review of implementation commit 46efbf3 for helix-bec526ad.\nReview target: last-commit resolved to commit:46efbf3 because HEAD 09f9367 is tracker-only close bookkeeping.\nReviewed scope: scripts/helix and tests/helix-cli.sh.\nGoverning execution bead: helix-bec526ad.","id":"helix-883e59e0","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:review","kind:planning","action:review","area:cli","area:testing","area:workflow"],"notes":"\u003cmeasure-results\u003e\n  \u003ctimestamp\u003e2026-04-11T06:03:00Z\u003c/timestamp\u003e\n  \u003cstatus\u003eISSUES_FOUND\u003c/status\u003e\n  \u003cfindings total=\"1\" filed=\"1\" critical=\"0\" high=\"0\" medium=\"1\" low=\"0\"/\u003e\n  \u003cagents-md-updated\u003eNO\u003c/agents-md-updated\u003e\n  \u003clearnings-filed\u003e0\u003c/learnings-filed\u003e\n\u003c/measure-results\u003e","owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"helix-bec526ad","status":"closed","title":"review: helix-bec526ad implementation commit 46efbf3","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:59:31.601348998Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:276:{"acceptance":"All tracker-evidence and validator findings for this area are classified with evidence and reflected in the durable report","created_at":"2026-04-11T06:40:08.442222037Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\n\u003cprinciples\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\u003c/principles\u003e\n\u003cconcerns\u003ehugo-hextra | demo-asciinema\u003c/concerns\u003e\n\u003cpractices\u003eDefine the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the \"first 5 minutes\" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)\u003c/practices\u003e\n\u003cadrs\u003eADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eworkflows/actions/report.md — helix report area:auth --since 2026-04-01\u003c/governing\u003e\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nReview tracker evidence durability, measure/report closure behavior, and deterministic validator coverage for key HELIX contract claims.","id":"helix-8b3b4643","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:review","kind:review","area:testing","area:workflow"],"parent":"helix-fd9c1ef7","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"workflows/actions/report.md","status":"closed","title":"Alignment review: repo - tracker evidence and validator contract","updated_at":"2026-04-11T06:48:23.188772699Z"}

The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.\u003c/adrs\u003e\n\u003cgoverning\u003eFEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector=\"${1:-}\" local context_file=\"${target_root}/.helix/context.md\" cat \u0026lt;\u0026lt;EOF $(if [[ -f \"$context_file\" ]]; then cat \"$context_file\"; printf '\\n---\\n\\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action \"${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md\") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Use \\ddx bead ready --json\\, \\ddx bead show \u0026lt;id\u0026gt;\\, \\ddx bead update \u0026lt;id\u0026gt; --claim\\, \\ddx bead close \u0026lt;id\u0026gt;\\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Reproduction from this review: create a temp `.ddx/beads.jsonl` containing malformed JSON, then run `HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 DDX_BEAD_DIR=\u003ctemp\u003e bash scripts/helix build --dry-run hx-bad` or `... build hx-bad`; both exit 1 with the selector-miss message instead of surfacing the tracker parse failure.\nSuggested fix: Preserve real resolver failures for explicit selectors by distinguishing \"no match\" from \"resolution failed\". `area:*` labels. This catches ADRs not yet wired into concerns.
   - For each relevant ADR: extract the decision statement and one-line
     rationale. Do not include alternatives or exploration.

5. **Governing spec**: Load the artifact referenced by `spec-id`.
   Extract:
   - The specific requirement or acceptance criterion this bead addresses
   - Key constraints relevant to implementation
   - Do not include the full spec — just the governing clause.

6. **Format**: Assemble into XML-tagged block (see format below).

7. **Write**: Prepend the `<context-digest>` block to the bead's
   `description` field. If a digest already exists, replace it.
   If the repository ships a digest helper script, use it instead of
   hand-assembling XML so the live queue and future beads stay consistent.
   When the governing contract explicitly permits omitting the digest,
   the bead must carry label `digest:omission-authorized`, the machine-set
   field `digest-omission-path`, and a description that begins with
   `Explicit omission rationale: <reason>` instead of a `<context-digest>`
   block. The rationale must be non-empty and explain why omission is
   allowed for that bead.
   HELIX currently defines exactly one allowed omission path:
   `helix-input:legacy-migration`. This path is reserved for `helix input`
   when it creates or updates a migrated legacy bead whose upstream concern
   mapping is not yet complete enough to assemble a trustworthy digest.
   Any other bead class must carry a full `<context-digest>`.

## Digest Format

```xml
<context-digest>
<principles>Principle 1 · Principle 2 · Principle 3</principles>
<concerns>Concern 1 | Concern 2 | Concern 3</concerns>
<practices>Practice 1 · Practice 2 · Practice 3</practices>
<adrs>ADR-NNN decision summary · ADR-NNN decision summary</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-NNN §X.Y — key requirement or constraint</governing>
</context-digest>
```

Each XML element is optional — omit it if there is no relevant content
(e.g., omit `<adrs>` if no ADRs are relevant, omit `<concerns>` if no
concerns are declared).

## Token Budget

| Section | Target | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Principles | ~100 tokens | Full list, compact format |
| Concerns | ~50 tokens | Matched concern names only |
| Practices | ~200 tokens | Key conventions, not exhaustive |

data.text: The validator leaves no shortcut: this bead can’t use an omission rationale, so it needs a real `<context-digest>`. I’m checking whether the repo already ships a digest refresher for open beads so I can fix the tracker metadata the sanctioned way instead of hand-editing JSONL.

candidates.extend(iter_markdown_clauses(extract_section(markdown, heading)))
    if not candidates:
        candidates = iter_markdown_clauses(markdown)
    clause = choose_best_clause(candidates, query)
    if clause:
        return f"{artifact_id} — {clause}"
    return artifact_id


def build_digest(
    item: dict,
    principles: list[str],
    library: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]],
    root: Path,
    library_root: Path | None = None,
) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    library_root = library_root or root
    existing_digest, body = split_digest(item.get("description", ""))
    labels = infer_area_labels(item)
    bead_areas = {label.removeprefix("area:") for label in labels}
    matched = [
        name
        for name, data in library.items()
        if concern_matches(bead_areas, data["areas"])
    ]
    adrs = build_adrs(root, item, labels, matched, library, library_root)
    practices = []
    for name in matched:
        practices.extend(
            select_digest_practices(
                library[name]["library_practices"],
                library[name]["override_practices"],
                limit=2,
            )
        )
    extra_tags = extract_extra_tags(existing_digest)

    digest_lines = ["<context-digest>"]
    if principles:
        digest_lines.append(
            f"<principles>{html.escape(' · '.join(principles), quote=False)}</principles>"
        )
    if matched:
        digest_lines.append(
            f"<concerns>{html.escape(' | '.join(compact(matched, 5)), quote=False)}</concerns>"
        )
    if practices:
        digest_lines.append(
            f"<practices>{html.escape(' · '.join(compact(practices, 6)), quote=False)}</practices>"
        )
    if adrs:
        digest_lines.append(f"<adrs>{html.escape(' · '.join(adrs), quote=False)}</adrs>")
    governing = build_governing(root, item, library_root)
    if governing:
        digest_lines.append(f"<governing>{html.escape(governing, quote=False)}</governing>")
    digest_lines.extend(extra_tags)
    digest_lines.append("</context-digest>")

    rebuilt_description = "\n".join(digest_lines)
    if body:
        rebuilt_description += "\n\n" + body
    return rebuilt_description, labels


def run_update(bead_id: str, description: str, labels: list[str]) -> None:
    subprocess.run(
        [
            "ddx",
            "bead",
            "update",
            bead_id,
            "--description",
            description,
            "--labels",
            ",".join(labels),
        ],
        check=True,
    )


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Refresh HELIX bead context digests and area labels.")
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Write updates back to the tracker.")
    parser.add_argument("--bead", action="append", default=[], help="Restrict updates to specific bead IDs.")
    parser.add_argument("--root", help="Project root containing the target tracker and docs.")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--library-root",
        help="HELIX library root containing workflows/ and scripts/ resources.",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--tracker", help="Explicit tracker path. Defaults to <root>/.ddx/beads.jsonl.")
    parser.add_argument("--status", default="open", help="Tracker status to target (default: open).")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    root = Path(args.root).resolve() if args.root else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    library_root = (
        Path(args.library_root).resolve()
        if args.library_root
        else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    )
    tracker = Path(args.tracker).resolve() if args.tracker else root / ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
    concerns_path = root / "docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md"
    concerns_doc = read_text(concerns_path) if concerns_path.exists() else ""
    active_concerns = parse_active_concerns(concerns_doc)
    overrides = parse_overrides(concerns_doc)
    principles = parse_principles(root, library_root)
    library = build_concern_library(root, active_concerns, overrides, library_root)

    targeted = set(args.bead)
    changed = 0
    for line in read_text(tracker).splitlines():
        if not line.strip():
            continue
        item = json.loads(line)
        if item.get("status") != args.status:
            continue
        if targeted and item.get("id") not in targeted:
            continue
        new_description, new_labels = build_digest(
            item,
            principles,
            library,
            root,
            library_root,
        )
        old_labels = [label for label in item.get("labels", []) if label.startswith("area:")]
        if new_description == item.get("description", "") and sorted(old_labels) == new_labels:
            continue
        changed += 1
        print(f"{item['id']}: {','.join(old_labels) or '-'} -> {','.join(new_labels) or '-'}")
        if args.apply:
            merged_labels = [label for label in item.get("labels", []) if not label.startswith("area:")] + new_labels
            run_update(item["id"], new_description, merged_labels)
    if not changed:
        print("No bead updates required.")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.architecture — workflows/ is what adopters install; docs/helix/ demonstrates the methodology applied to HELIX's own development. The Hugo microsite (when generated) is a read-only projection of both trees, not a source of truth.</governing>
</context-digest>

The script `scripts/publish-artifacts.py` publishes `docs/helix` to `website/content/artifacts`.

Out-of-scope:

- Do not change microsite design or navigation beyond projection correctness.
